steps.text: Introduce a solvent solution (lixiviant) through injection wells drilled into the ore zone to leach uranium from underground ore bodies.
Pump the uranium-bearing solution to the surface via extraction wells.
Process the solution to extract the uranium.
Keep the other Party generally informed of its relevant plans and schedules.
Respond to the other Party's requests for information to the extent reasonable and practical.
Strive to recognize and minimize any problems arising from each Party's regulatory activities in implementing this MOU.
Respond to the other Party's requests for information to the extent reasonable and practical, and strive to recognize and minimize any problems arising from each Party's regulatory activities.
Make staff members of the Parties readily available for collaboration, information sharing, and resolution of each Party's concerns.
Work in good faith to resolve concerns in a timely manner and avoid undue delays.
Treat the drafts of any proposed or final rules, including any preambles thereof, and any other related documents or information, as pre-decisional and deliberative information.
Ensure standards of general application intended to control radiological hazards are in the form of limits on radiation exposures or levels, or concentrations or quantities of radioactive material.

Ensure standards of general application intended to control non-radiological hazards provide for the protection of human health and the environment consistent with the standards required under subtitle C of the Solid Waste Disposal Act (SWDA).
Consult with the NRC.
Promulgate standards of general application.
Apply the existing groundwater protection standards currently set forth in 40 CFR Part 192, Subpart D.

Request the concurrence of the EPA in writing from the Director, Office of Nuclear Material Safety and Safeguards, to the EPA through the Director, Office of Radiation and Indoor Air, including a copy of the NRC's draft final rule.
Make all reasonable efforts to resolve any disagreement concerning the EPA's concurrence collegially and quickly.
Raise the resolution of the disagreement to the Office of the Executive Director for Operations for the NRC and to the Assistant Administrator for Air and Radiation for the EPA.
Designate a representative and alternate representative to ensure coordination.
Provide written notice to the other Party to change its representative.
Terminate participation in this MOU.
Execute this MOU on the dates shown.

preconditions.text: In carrying out their respective responsibilities
EPA chooses to promulgate standards of general application under its authority in AEA § 275
EPA plans to promulgate standards of general application.
NRC chooses to promulgate regulations for regulating ISR activities.
EPA has not yet promulgated new standards of general application under AEA § 275.
NRC rulemaking would promulgate general requirements for the management of byproduct material
A disagreement arises between the Parties concerning the EPA's concurrence regarding the NRC's draft final rule containing the general requirements for the management of byproduct material.
It is necessary to escalate the disagreement because it was not resolved collegially and quickly.
Provide written notice to the other Party at least 30 days in advance of the desired termination date.

constraints.text: Without proper controls, altered groundwater can potentially migrate from the production zone to groundwater outside of the ISR wellfield.

Neither Party will disclose or transmit the draft regulations or other related documents or information to any third party unless expressly agreed to by the other Party or as required by applicable law.
Standards of general application must not direct the use of a particular, detailed management, engineering, or technical method or test to demonstrate licensee compliance.
Standards of general application must not restrict the regulatory agency's selection of an appropriate methodology or test to monitor licensee compliance.

Use the corrective action framework under Subtitle C of the SWDA as the model for developing standards of general application concerning or relating to those aquifers in ISR wellfields that have been restored to approved groundwater protection standards.
The Parties will endeavor to resolve all disagreements prior to publication of the final rule in the Federal Register.

Any transaction involving transfers of funds between the Parties pursuant to this MOU will be handled in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, and procedures under separate written agreements.
This MOU does not obligate any funding.
Each Party retains all immunities and defenses provided by law with respect to any action based on or occurring as a result of this MOU.
